operations.value: Exercise trials use diverse acute and short-term Rhodiola protocols and report mixed effects on endurance, perceived exertion, power, muscle damage, and recovery; current evidence does not support a broad claim that Rhodiola reliably improves athletic performance.
operations.notes: Reflects heterogeneous protocols and outcomes across systematic reviews while avoiding a generalized ergogenic claim.

sources.title: Rhodiola rosea supplementation on sports performance: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials
Effects of Rhodiola Rosea Supplementation on Exercise and Sport: A Systematic Review
Rhodiola rosea for physical and mental fatigue: a systematic review

notes: Adds a conservative exercise-performance interpretation rather than a product-level performance promise.
